notableMoments: Haiti's first World Cup appearance came in 1974 in West Germany, where Emmanuel Sanon scored a historic goal to break Italian goalkeeper Dino Zoff's world-record unbeaten streak of 1,142 minutes
The 2026 World Cup marks Haiti's return to the tournament after 52 years, the longest gap between appearances for any nation in the tournament's history
Emmanuel Sanon's goal against Italy in 1974 is considered one of the most iconic moments in Caribbean football history
Haiti qualified for the 2026 World Cup through the CONCACAF pathway, a remarkable achievement for a nation that has overcome immense socio-economic challenges
The 1974 squad was celebrated as national heroes upon their return, despite losing all three group matches against Italy, Poland, and Argentina
Haiti's football resurgence represents one of the most inspiring stories in the 2026 World Cup, demonstrating the sport's power to unite communities through adversity
The current squad blends players from MLS, French lower divisions, and the domestic Ligue Haitienne, reflecting the Haitian diaspora's contribution to the national team
Frantzdy Pierrot and Derrick Etienne Jr. lead a new generation of Haitian footballers aiming to write a more successful chapter than the 1974 campaign
